title: Push for super fund access to Centrelink
full_text:
Big super is calling on the Albanese government to give funds access to Centrelink, with Cbus chairman Wayne Swan backing “close co-operation” between the public social security system and the nation’s $4.3 trillion retirement nest egg.
Anthony Albanese is also facing demands from the Coalition to explain what changes he discussed with Paul Keating, after the former Labor prime minister revealed they had spoken about a “big opportunity” regarding Centrelink, the age pension and super.
Labor MPs on Wednesday said they believed information sharing between Centrelink and super funds had merit if it helped people better understand what they were entitled to in retirement, though they held concerns about privacy.
Mr Swan said allowing super funds access to Centrelink would benefit members entitled to the age pension, but declined to comment on whether he would support Centrelink being merged with super.
